promotion.context: Playoff residual calibrator. Trained on v1 playoff resim cache (13,186 legs, 9 dates 2026-04-30..2026-05-08). All architectural fixtures from upstream work carry forward.

architectural_fixtures.p_for_cal_source: p_adj (engine fork bypassed in cache; engine main.py:911 still has the buggy fork pending replay-validated removal)
architectural_fixtures.use_role_flag: binary indicator: role_ctx_outs_used > 0; lets calibrator branch per leg
architectural_fixtures.te_source: player_te_lookup.json (corrected source verified to match inference)

exclude_rationale.2026-05-02: Single-game slate (628 legs); too narrow to evaluate as independent fold; aggregate distortion expected.
exclude_rationale.2026-05-04: Role-context churn day; share matrix had transient instability driving high baseline noise across stats.
exclude_rationale.2026-05-06: Wrong-side blowout: market closed MIN -9.5 favored; SAS won by 38. Single matchup MIN@SAS = 54% of slate (777/1434 legs). Ayo Dosunmu played 10 minutes 0/0/0 vs minutes_s=1.0 projection. q_blowout symmetric on both teams, no upstream signal could distinguish favorite-side collapse. Diagnosed in tools/diagnose_2026-05-06_kernel_inputs.py.

gate.definition: PROMOTE iff (clean_agg_delta_mB < -0.5) AND (every clean fold's delta <= gate-by-size)

v5c-D_iter600.note: Statistically equivalent to v5c-A. best_iter=595-599 across folds (model wanted more rounds but extra rounds did not improve metrics). v5c-A iter=500 selected for parsimony.

v5c-B_scale060.note: Stronger residual scale buys aggregate but breaks worst-slate gate.

v5c-C_scale070.note: Even stronger; more aggregate at higher worst-slate cost. Reserve for future when corpus expands.

outstanding_actions: Replay-validate the cache-side fix (p_for_cal := p_adj + use_role flag) before modifying engine main.py:911.
Refresh playoff corpus with 2-3 additional dates to dilute clean-6 exclusion ratio (currently 3/9 = 33% excluded).
Investigate raw-side: MC kernel (p) outperforms feature-driven layer (p_for_cal) on playoff data; possible signal that GBM features carry regular-season bias that does not transfer.
